{"id":"8b01baa2-46ef-4064-9fc2-e832d89458e9","identifier":"FRG-177","title":"v2 Phase 3: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages + Workers","description":"## Context

Phases 0-2 merged. App is functional on Vercel. Need to deploy to Cloudflare Pages + Workers for commercial use (Vercel Hobby ToS prohibits commercial).

## Requirements

### 1\. Configure Nitro for Cloudflare

* In `nuxt.config.ts`, set `nitro.preset` to `cloudflare-pages` (or `cloudflare_pages`)
* Verify the server route `/api/format` works on Cloudflare Workers runtime
* Note: DOMParser is NOT available in Workers — we already use htmlparser2 (fixed in Phase 0)
* Note: DOMPurify runs client-side only (lazy imported), so no Workers issue there

### 2\. Wrangler Configuration

* Create `wrangler.toml` if needed for environment variables
* `OPENAI_API_KEY` must be set as a secret (not in code): `npx wrangler secret put OPENAI_API_KEY`
* Or use Cloudflare dashboard to set env vars

### 3\. Deploy

* Build: `npx nuxt build`
* Deploy: `npx wrangler pages deploy .output/public` (or use Cloudflare Pages Git integration)
* Verify the deployed app works end-to-end (paste text → format → preview → export)

### 4\. Auto-deploy (optional)

* Connect GitHub repo to Cloudflare Pages for auto-deploy on push to main
* Build command: `npx nuxt build`
* Output directory: `.output/public`

## Important

* Keep Vercel deploy working too (for now) — don't break existing config
* The `nuxt.config.ts` change to cloudflare preset may affect local dev — test with `npx nuxt dev` after

## Git

1→import { Parser } from 'htmlparser2'
     2→
     3→const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a professional document formatter. Convert plain-text resumes and cover letters into structured XML.
     4→
     5→For resumes use this schema:
     6→<document type="resume">
     7→  <header>
     8→    <name>Full Name</name>
     9→    <contact>
    10→      <email>email@example.com</email>
    11→      <phone>555-000-0000</phone>
    12→      <location>City, State</location>
    13→      <linkedin>linkedin.com/in/username</linkedin>
    14→      <website>portfolio.com</website>
    15→    </contact>
    16→  </header>
    17→  <summary>Professional summary text.</summary>
    18→  <experience>
    19→    <role>
    20→      <title>Job Title</title>
    21→      <company>Company Name</company>
    22→      <period>Month Year – Month Year</period>
    23→      <bullets>
    24→        <bullet>Key achievement or responsibility</bullet>
    25→      </bullets>
    26→    </role>
    27→  </experience>
    28→  <education>
    29→    <degree>Degree Name</degree>
    30→    <school>School Name</school>
    31→    <year>Graduation Year</year>
    32→    <note>Honors or relevant note</note>
    33→  </education>
    34→  <skills>Skill 1, Skill 2, Skill 3</skills>
    35→  <certifications>Cert 1, Cert 2</certifications>
    36→  <projects>Project descriptions</projects>
    37→  <volunteer>Volunteer experience</volunteer>
    38→</document>
    39→
    40→For cover letters use type="cover-letter" and adapt content into letter sections using summary (opening), experience (body paragraphs), and closing elements.
    41→
    42→Rules:
    43→- Preserve ALL information from the source text
    44→- Omit empty optional tags
    45→- Output ONLY valid XML — no markdown fences, no explanation
    46→- Detect whether the input is a resume or cover letter and set the type attribute accordingly`
    47→
    48→interface OpenAIChatResponse {
    49→  choices: Array<{
    50→    message: {
    51→      content: string
    52→    }
    53→  }>
    54→}
    55→
    56→function extractTextFromXml(xmlString: string): string {
    57→  const textParts: string[] = []
    58→  const parser = new Parser({
    59→    ontext(text) {
    60→      textParts.push(text)
    61→    },
    62→  }, { decodeEntities: true })
    63→  parser.write(xmlString)
    64→  parser.end()
    65→  return textParts.join(' ')
    66→}
    67→
    68→function detectDocumentType(xmlString: string): string {
    69→  const match = xmlString.match(/<document[^>]+type="([^"]+)"/)
    70→  return match?.[1] ?? 'resume'
    71→}
    72→
    73→function validateXmlStructure(xmlString: string): boolean {
    74→  let valid = true
    75→  let depth = 0
    76→  const parser = new Parser({
    77→    onerror() {
    78→      valid = false
    79→    },
    80→    onopentag() {
    81→      depth++
    82→    },
    83→    onclosetag() {
    84→      depth--
    85→      if (depth < 0) valid = false
    86→    },
    87→  }, { xmlMode: true })
    88→  parser.write(xmlString)
    89→  parser.end()
    90→  return valid && depth === 0
    91→}
    92→
    93→export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
    94→  const body = await readBody(event)
    95→
    96→  if (!body.text || body.text.length < 50) {
    97→    throw createError({ statusCode: 400, message: 'Paste more content to format.' })
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  const config = useRuntimeConfig()
   101→
   102→  if (!config.openaiApiKey) {
   103→    throw createError({ statusCode: 500, message: 'OpenAI API key is not configured.' })
   104→  }
   105→
   106→  const inputLen = body.text.length
   107→
   108→  const response = await $fetch<OpenAIChatResponse>('https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions', {
   109→    method: 'POST',
   110→    headers: {
   111→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.openaiApiKey}`,
   112→      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   113→    },
   114→    body: {
   115→      model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
   116→      messages: [
   117→        { role: 'system', content: SYSTEM_PROMPT },
   118→        { role: 'user', content: body.text },
   119→      ],
   120→      temperature: 0.3,
   121→      max_tokens: 4000,
   122→    },
   123→  })
   124→
   125→  const raw = response.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? ''
   126→
   127→  // Strip markdown fences if present
   128→  const xml = raw
   129→    .replace(/^```(?:xml)?\n?/m, '')
   130→    .replace(/\n?```$/m, '')
   131→    .trim()
   132→
   133→  if (!validateXmlStructure(xml)) {
   134→    throw createError({ statusCode: 502, message: 'AI returned malformed XML. Please try again.' })
   135→  }
   136→
   137→  const xmlText = extractTextFromXml(xml)
   138→  const xmlTextLen = xmlText.length
   139→  const divergence = Math.abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen
   140→
   141→  const documentType = detectDocumentType(xml)
   142→
   143→  return { xml, divergence, documentType }
   144→})
   145→

Actually, I'm overcomplicating this. Since Nitro already reads the `NITRO_PRESET` environment variable directly, adding it to the config would just create redundancy. ## Summary
- Add `nitro.preset = 'cloudflare-pages'` to `nuxt.config.ts` so Nuxt/Nitro targets the Cloudflare Pages + Workers runtime at build time

**Build:**
```bash
npx nuxt build
```

**Deploy via Wrangler CLI:**
```bash
npx wrangler pages deploy dist
```

```bash
npx wrangler secret put OPENAI_API_KEY
```

**Or via Cloudflare Pages Git Integration:**
- Connect GitHub repo in the Cloudflare Pages dashboard
- Build command: `npx nuxt build`
- Output directory: `dist`
- Add `OPENAI_API_KEY` under Settings → Environment variables (use "Secret" type)

Closes FRG-177

## Implementation complete — PR #33

**Branch:** `feature/frg-177-cloudflare-deploy`
**PR:** https://github.com/kimwwk/ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent/pull/33

### What was done

**`nuxt.config.ts`** — Added `nitro.preset = 'cloudflare-pages'` so Nuxt/Nitro builds for the Cloudflare Pages + Workers runtime. A comment explains that Vercel's Git integration automatically overrides this with `NITRO_PRESET=vercel`, keeping Vercel deploys unaffected.

**`wrangler.toml`** (new file) — Cloudflare Pages configuration:
- `name = "ohmydoc"`
- `compatibility_date = "2025-07-15"` (matches Nuxt config)
- `compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"]` — required for `htmlparser2` and Nitro's Node.js APIs in Workers runtime
- `pages_build_output_dir = "dist"` — the actual Nitro output directory (verified via build)
- Guidance for setting `OPENAI_API_KEY` as a secret (never committed)

### Verified

- `nuxt build` succeeds and produces Cloudflare-specific output: `dist/_worker.js`, `dist/_routes.json`, `dist/_headers`, `dist/_redirects`
- `/api/format` route compiled into `dist/_worker.js/chunks/routes/api/format.post.mjs`
- `htmlparser2` bundled correctly (no DOMParser usage)
- No linting errors

### Deployment steps for Cloudflare

```bash
npx nuxt build
npx wrangler secret put OPENAI_API_KEY   # one-time
npx wrangler pages deploy dist
```

Or connect the GitHub repo to Cloudflare Pages with build command `npx nuxt build` and output directory `dist`.

Phases 0-2 merged. App is functional on Vercel. Need to deploy to Cloudflare Pages + Workers for commercial use (Vercel Hobby ToS prohibits commercial).

## Requirements

### 1\. Configure Nitro for Cloudflare

* In `nuxt.config.ts`, set `nitro.preset` to `cloudflare-pages` (or `cloudflare_pages`)
* Verify the server route `/api/format` works on Cloudflare Workers runtime
* Note: DOMParser is NOT available in Workers — we already use htmlparser2 (fixed in Phase 0)
* Note: DOMPurify runs client-side only (lazy imported), so no Workers issue there

### 2\. Wrangler Configuration

* Create `wrangler.toml` if needed for environment variables
* `OPENAI_API_KEY` must be set as a secret (not in code): `npx wrangler secret put OPENAI_API_KEY`
* Or use Cloudflare dashboard to set env vars

### 3\. Deploy

* Build: `npx nuxt build`
* Deploy: `npx wrangler pages deploy .output/public` (or use Cloudflare Pages Git integration)
* Verify the deployed app works end-to-end (paste text → format → preview → export)

### 4\. Auto-deploy (optional)

* Connect GitHub repo to Cloudflare Pages for auto-deploy on push to main
* Build command: `npx nuxt build`
* Output directory: `.output/public`

## Important

* Keep Vercel deploy working too (for now) — don't break existing config
* The `nuxt.config.ts` change to cloudflare preset may affect local dev — test with `npx nuxt dev` after

## Git

* Branch from main, commit as `Chill <chill.kim.test@gmail.com>`, PR to main","priority":{"value":2,"name":"High"},"url":"https://linear.app/chills-linear/issue/FRG-177/v2-phase-3-deploy-to-cloudflare-pages-workers","gitBranchName":"chillkimtest/frg-177-v2-phase-3-deploy-to-cloudflare-pages-workers","createdAt":"2026-03-03T06:35:09.646Z","updatedAt":"2026-03-03T10:13:03.072Z","archivedAt":null,"completedAt":"2026-03-03T10:13:03.051Z","dueDate":null,"slaStartedAt":null,"slaMediumRiskAt":null,"slaHighRiskAt":null,"slaBreachesAt":null,"slaType":"all","status":"Done","labels":[],"attachments":[],"documents":[],"createdBy":"Chill","createdById":"b5a6e03a-2da1-4e78-951b-81497e2a00f7","project":"OhMyDoc","projectId":"ef0db844-5e09-4fa4-9268-d21aeed33fed","team":"Forge","teamId":"c3ef97d8-21a8-4aa7-a818-298f217864dd"}

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `nuxt.config.ts` — Added `nitro: { preset: 'cloudflare-pages' }` with a comment explaining that Vercel Git integration auto-overrides this via `NITRO_PRESET=vercel`
- `wrangler.toml` (new) — Cloudflare Pages config: `nodejs_compat` flag (needed for `htmlparser2`), `pages_build_output_dir = "dist"` (verified actual Nitro output path), and guidance for setting `OPENAI_API_KEY` as a Wrangler secret

## Key Decisions
- **Output dir is `dist/`** not `.output/public` — the issue docs said `.output/public` but the actual Cloudflare Pages preset outputs to `dist/`. Verified by running the build.
- **`nodejs_compat` flag** added — `htmlparser2` and Nitro internals require Node.js APIs not available in the base Workers runtime; this flag enables them without changing any application code.
- **Vercel compatibility** — Left Vercel working by relying on Vercel's build system automatically injecting `NITRO_PRESET=vercel`, which takes priority over the config value.

## PR / Branch
- Branch: `feature/frg-177-cloudflare-deploy`
- PR: https://github.com/kimwwk/ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent/pull/33
